---
title: "Worksheet 8"
author: "Fred Azizi"
date: "2022-10-26"
output: pdf_document
---

1. The number of flaws in an optic fiber cable follows a Poisson Distribution. The average number of flaws in $50 \mathrm{~m}$ is $1.5$. Let $x=$ average number of  flaws in $50 \mathrm{~m}$.

> a. What is the probability of exactly 2 flaws in $100 \mathrm{~m}$ ?

> b. What is the probability of 3 flaws or less in $150 \mathrm{~m}$ ?

\newpage


2. $X$ is a Uniformly distributed random variable that has maximum and minimum values of 10 and 50.

> a. What is the density function and its graph?

> b. Calculate the mean of $X$.

> c. Calculate $P[10<X<20]$ and mark the corresponding area on the graph from (a).

> d. Calculate $P[25<X<45]$ and mark the corresponding area on the graph from (a).

> e. Find $P[X=22.5]$.

\newpage

3. Calculate the following Probabilities from using a standard Normal distribution, $Z$ $\sim$ N(0,1).

> a. $P[Z < 0]$.

> b. $P[Z < -1.5]$.

> c. $P[Z > 1]$.

> d. $P[Z > -1.44]$.

> e. $P[Z > 3.09]$.

> f. $P[-0.71 < Z < 0.92]$.

> g. $P[-1.5 < Z < -1.44]$.

> h. $P[Z < -0.71 or Z > 0.92]$.

\newpage

4. Find values of $z$ such that:

> a. The area to the left is 0.0202.

> b. The area to the left is 0.7517.

> c. The area to the right is 0.025.

> d. The area to the right is 0.3015.
